content.full_text:
State of California

Memorandum

To    :  File                                                    Date  :  August 13, 1982

                                                                 File No. M-82-148


From  :  FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
         Janis Shank [illegible]

Subject:  Section 82015

         Facts:  The Building Industry Association invited
         4 out of 12 candidates running for SMUD Board positions
         to speak at their General Membership meeting.  The
         B.I.A. does not have to report this as a contribution
         to the candidates.

         Rationale:  Because the meeting is held for a purpose
         other than just to listen to the candidates, and since
         there is no additional expense incurred to have the
         candidates there, the B.I.A. need not report this as a
         contribution.  Also, people are free to choose whether
         or not to attend.

         JSM:plh

sections.question_synthetic: Whether the Building Industry Association must report as a contribution the invitation and speaking opportunity provided to 4 out of 12 candidates for SMU Board positions at their General Membership meeting.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The B.I.A. does not have to report this as a contribution because the meeting serves a purpose other than just listening to candidates, no additional expense is incurred, and attendance is voluntary.
sections.parsing_notes: Format: modern; LLM: Document is a memorandum rather than traditional advice letter format. No formal QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections present; analysis structured as Facts and Rationale. File number M-82-148 corresponds to document ID 82A148.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether the Building Industry Association must report as a contribution the invitation and speaking opportunity provided to 4 out of 12 candidates for SMU Board positions at their General Membership meeting.

CONCLUSION: The B.I.A. does not have to report this as a contribution because the meeting serves a purpose other than just listening to candidates, no additional expense is incurred, and attendance is voluntary.

The B.I.A. does not have to report this as a contribution to the candidates. Rationale: Because the meeting is held for a purpose other than just to listen to the candidates, and since there is no additional expense incurred to have the candidates there, the B.I.A. need not report this as a contribution. Also, people are free to choose whether or not to attend. JSM:plh
embedding.summary: FPPC memorandum ruling that the Building Industry Association's invitation of 4 candidates to speak at their General Membership meeting does not constitute a reportable contribution under Section 82015.
